text.title: Design Document Structure and Role
text.summary: What a design document is, how it differs from spec and implementation plan, and the canonical section list for design.md v2.0.

section.title: Document Role
section.p: Design document answers: HOW are we building this? Spec answers: WHAT to build and WHY. Implementation plan answers: in what ORDER, by whom.

list.item: Spec (specification): question=What to build? Audience=stakeholders, PMs. Content=requirements, AC. Output=FR-X, NFR-X.
Design (design doc): question=How to build it? Audience=developers, agents. Content=architecture, APIs. Output=AD-X, file list.
Impl Plan: question=In what order? Audience=developers, agents. Content=tasks, estimates. Output=TASK-XXX list.

section.title: Document Hierarchy
section.p: CONSTITUTION (project-wide) → informs SPEC (feature) → informs DESIGN (feature) → informs IMPL PLAN → TASKS

rule.statement: A design document must exist and be approved before the implementation plan is written.
rule.rationale: The file change table in design.md is the input to task decomposition in the implementation plan. Without an approved design, the implementation plan has no authoritative file list, and tasks will be created for files that may not be needed.

list.item: Reference Documents: links to spec, constitution, related designs
Spec Coverage: FR-X → AD-X traceability matrix
Architectural Decisions: AD-X with context, decision, rationale, alternatives, consequences
File Structure: CREATE/MODIFY table with FR and AD traces per row
Data Models: TypeScript types + SQL DDL (kept separate — they often diverge)
API Contracts: endpoints with OpenAPI-style request/response
Dependencies: packages with versions, external services
Security Considerations: specific threats with mitigations
Testing Strategy: unit, integration, E2E targets
Migration Strategy: if applicable — data migration + rollback plan

text.reference: Canonical design.md v2.0 template with all sections
Verify spec FR → design AD coverage and file table traces
